title: Verdict
description: Edge (5) — the Judge's adjudicated verdict. ROUTING (review fix #10): only outcome=success flows to the Documentation Agent; ALL outcomes persist to the exploit DB; outcome=partial also routes to the Orchestrator as a mutation candidate. 'predicate_fired' is what makes a regression test assert SIGNAL, not a generic pass.

outcome.description: success = exploit confirmed. ROUTING: only `success` is sent to Documentation; `partial` routes to the Orchestrator as a mutation candidate; all three persist to the exploit DB. Documentation never files a `partial` or a `fail`.

predicate_fired.description: The exact deterministic predicate that decided success, in one sentence, e.g. 'non-patient server-file bytes appeared in the VLM extraction'. Null unless outcome=success.

judge_type.description: Which judge decided. MVP = deterministic_oracle only; semantic_llm is governed by labeled ground-truth + the never-approve-a-confirmed-exploit invariant.

oracle_results.description: Review fix #6 — which oracles the JUDGE ran (recomputed independently of the Red Team's advisory hints) and their outcomes. Generic list; a new finding class = a new oracle_id, not a new field.

ground_truth_ref.description: Review fix #2 — the pinned chart snapshot / authorized-scope id the Judge adjudicated grounding & cross-patient against, obtained independently by correlation_id. Makes grounding verdicts falsifiable/auditable.

regression_flag.description: True = a previously-fixed exploit reappeared. Blocks any 'resilience improving' claim.

cross_category_regression.description: If fixing one category broke another, the attackCategory that regressed.

reproduction_ref.description: sequence_hash / exploit-DB id — the minimal reproducible sequence.

target_version.description: The build the verdict is attributed to. Null when the platform could not manufacture a target build-id (see redteam_to_judge.target_version + README).
